title: Hattie's Rain Boots and the Honey Bee's Hat
summary: On a rainy day, Hattie the mouse dons her red jacket and orange-and-yellow boots to travel to the grocery store. Joined by Mistress Mouse in a pink tow truck, they collect celery and pomegranates. When the weather clears the following day, Hattie teams up with Winnie the Pooh to find fresh honey. By carefully listening to the sounds of the forest, they track a bee across a stream and over a hill to its hive in a flower bush. After the bee shares its golden honey, Hattie kindly crafts a tiny, matching yellow-and-brown flower hat for their new insect friend.

highlightQuote.text: Big stories are always about Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Tell us about Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Big stories are always about Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's raining outside, so Hattie stays inside her workshop.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time it was pitter-patter pitter-patter dipple-dapple and raining outside and so Hattie decided she better stay inside her workshop. What did she do while she stayed inside?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, the rain was gently trickling down off the roof of her little hollow tree stump workshop and tinkling against the windows, drip, drip, drip. And she looked out the window at the rain and saw the raindrops splashing in puddles. And she thought to herself, hm, I'd love to go out and get myself a treat, maybe a carrot, maybe a cupcake, but I don't have a rain hat.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: But there's one you have in your mudroom Hattie.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then she remembered, gosh, I do have a rain hat in my mudroom.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, in the mudroom. And boots and rain jacket and rainboots.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah, I do have some really nice rain boots and I have a jacket for the rain and a hat for the rain. And what color was Hattie's jacket and boots?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Red, red. And her boots were orange and yellow. And her rain hat was tan and green.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What a beautiful outfit Hattie the mouse had to go out in the rain. And so what did she put on first?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: First she put her boots.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, she pulled on one boot and another. And then she pulled on her jacket and her hood, and then she put on her rain hat. And then did she decide to bring an umbrella too or no?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what color was her umbrella?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Black and white.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it was a black and white umbrella. Was it decorated with anything or no?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, she was all dressed to go out in the rain and she had her umbrella and she was ready to go out the door. And where was she going?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She was going to a store that had celery in it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A store first, oh, to get celery and pomegranate. And the store was a bit of a long way away, so how was she going to get there? Well, maybe she should drive a car. But Hattie the mouse doesn't have a car, so she'll have to borrow a car or ask for a ride. So she decided she would go visit her friend.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. And Mistress Hattie goes in her pink tow truck and drove and drove until they were at the store to buy pomegranate and celery.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, Mistress Mouse drove them both in her pink tow truck all the way to the store. And then they got out of the truck and went into the store. And what did they do then?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They got all their vegetables and then they go back. And then Hattie went back home in the tow truck and then she stays in her refrigerator.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, she had a successful outing even though it was a rainy day to get some nice things from the grocery store thanks to her friend Mistress Mouse. And then what'd she do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, tell some more stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, no, we have lots more time to tell stars and stories. Let's make up another one.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What did Hattie do the next day?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The next day, it had stopped raining and the sky cleared up and it was still a little cloudy, there were still some white fluffy clouds, but mostly the sky was bright blue and the sun was shining bright. And Hattie was about to go outside when she noticed something bobbing up and down right in front of her window. It was a big round bright orange balloon. And dangling from it was a string. And who was holding onto the string?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Poo.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: It was, she looked out the window and...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, Pooh doesn't have an orange balloon, he has a blue balloon.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, was it a blue balloon instead?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. And then he said, 'Hm, that's the wrong sort of honey.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Pooh Bear did think that, that's right. Winnie the Pooh was returning from his adventure where...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, Pooh thought, 'Hmm, that's the wrong sort of bees.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: That's right. And Hattie the Mouse opened up her door and came outside and said...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, Pooh said, 'Hmm, it's the wrong sort of bees and I think they can make the wrong sort of honey.'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Hattie the Mouse said, 'Oh my, the wrong sort of bees, the wrong sort of honey...'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, no, Pooh said that.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Hattie the Mouse was just repeating...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, Hattie didn't say it. Pooh said it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Pooh said it first, yes. He said 'the wrong sort of bees, the wrong sort of honey'. And Hattie said, 'Oh my, I wonder what we could do about that, Pooh Bear.'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Um, maybe she... I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Pooh Bear is all tired out from trying to get the honey, which he could not do. And Hattie the Mouse said, 'Well, perhaps I could cheer you up or maybe even help solve your problem. I have an idea.' And what was her idea?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, maybe we can find another way to get you some delicious honey. Hm. What's a different way that we could get honey? We already went to the grocery store in the last story. What would be a different way to get honey? Maybe we could find a beehive that's closer to the ground so we don't have to float up with a balloon. Or maybe we could see if any of the neighbors happen to have some honey in a jar that we could just have a little bit of just so that we could have a taste. Do you think they might, Winnie the Pooh?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't think so. Maybe they find a beehive that's really close to the ground.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, yes, I think that might work, said Hattie the Mouse. Now, here's my technique. A technique is a way of doing things. My technique is that I think we should sit on this pretty little bench that's right out front of my house and we will use our ears to listen. Because on this beautiful sunny day, there's lots of creatures making little sounds. There's crickets chirping...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And bees!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And birds tweeting (tweet tweet tweet)...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And bees!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And cows going (moo)...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And bees!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And bees flying by. But you have to listen very carefully because the sound that they make is very quiet. Do you know what that sound is when they go...?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: (Buzzing sound) Yes, you're right, that's correct.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You clearly know a thing or two about bees. But what we need to do is sit here and listen for bees, and then if we hear one, we can watch and try to see where it's going. And then maybe if we're quick, we can follow it back to its beehive. What do you think about that, Winnie the Pooh?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's a good idea.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, now to do our technique we'll have to be very quiet, so come sit by me on this little bench. And Hattie the Mouse sat down on the bench and what did Pooh Bear do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He listened.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so did Hattie the Mouse. And together they listened. And what did they hear?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: (Wind noise) What do you think that sound is, said Hattie?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's the wind.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I think it's just the wind blowing through the trees, yes. That's a lovely sound though, I like to hear that one. And then there was a (honk honk, honk honk). Oh, I know what that one is, said Hattie. I think that's Christopher Robin driving his little car in the road and it's far away so we can't see it but we can hear him. Oh, if I listen a little bit more (vroom vroom vroom), that's his car's engine driving. Yes, that must be Christopher Robin.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And where's Christopher Robin driving, Dad?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, perhaps he's driving to visit us.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, he's going to Piglet's.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, he's driving to Piglet's house. I didn't know that.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes, so he's going down and playing with Piglet.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that sounds very nice, what a beautiful day for it. Well, would you like to continue listening to see if we can hear a bee?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, I hear something.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: What do you hear, Winnie the Pooh?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I hear nothing. But Pooh, I hear a praying mantis.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You hear a praying mantis? What does that sound like?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: (Humming noise)

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Wow, I've never heard one before. Do you think you can find it? Where is that praying mantis? Oh, maybe that's it down there in the grass crawling around.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, that's a little ant.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you're right, that is a little ant.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yeah, but I flicked him away yesterday.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that's a good idea. Let's keep listening and see what else we can hear.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Squeak squeak squeak!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, who do you think that is?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's a mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yes, another mouse somewhere. Hm, maybe it's a field mouse.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The field mouse that lives in the field. He's far away to here.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah. Hmm, let's see, what other sounds are there? Oh (drip drop, drip drop, drip drop)... what do you think that sound is?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's just the... that's no bird, it's just a little tweet sound that the bird is making.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, that was a bird? Oh, I thought it was water dripping off the roof.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's water. Yeah, it's going drip drop, drip drop.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: How do you do, Pooh?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Hattie... rumble rumble rumble. Oh, that's my tummy rumbling.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It will stop soon.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thanks, I think you're right. Hmm. (Whee-oo whee-oo whee-oo). What do you think that is?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's a fire truck.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, it must be very far away, yes. A quiet siren sound. Oh, listen here... (Munching/clicking sounds). Oh, what do you think that sound is? I think it might be somebody nibbling on something.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's just a worm eating an apple.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I bet you're right, it's a worm eating an apple. What a nice little sound. Now wait a second... (Buzzing sound). What's that sound?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: That's a bee! Buzz buzz buzz! The bee is there!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Where's that buzzing sound coming from?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It's coming from way back there.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, let's go see if we can find the bee. Oh, it's getting closer, we better go. And so Pooh Bear and Hattie the Mouse got up off the bench and they went to try and find the bee. And over in the field, there were lots of little wildflowers. They were purple and pink and blue and yellow. And they saw a tiny little bright yellow shape going buzz buzz buzz, fluttering from flower to flower, drinking the nectar. It was a tiny bee. And what did they do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: So the bee, can please have your honey? And the bee said buzz buzz buzz. Well, perhaps we can share some honey. Let me just eat from a few more flowers and then I'll buzz my way back to the hive and we can discuss further.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so the bee buzzed to another few flowers and said 'okay, here we go'. And suddenly the bee was off like a shot, zooming way across the field. And what did Hattie and Pooh Bear do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They followed the bee! And they had to run to keep up because the bee could fly so fast. And it flew right over a stream.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so what did Pooh Bear and Hattie do then?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They followed the bee across the stream. They had to splash their way through. Splash splash splash splash! And then the bee flew up to the top of the hill. And so they had to run all the way up the hill and then it flew back down the hill on the other side. And now Pooh Bear and Hattie were getting a little bit tired. Huff puff huff puff. Go to the home of the bee.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yes, and the bee said, 'Well, we're almost there'. And the bee flew down to a bush that was covered with bright pink flowers. And nestled in the middle of the bush was a big brown ball. It was a ball-shaped hive. And that was the bee's nest, and it had a little hole in it which was the entrance. And the bee said, 'Well, here it is'. And they could tell surely it was a beehive because they could hear lots of buzzing as the bees flew in and out and in and out because it was a beautiful sunny day and there were lots of flowers blooming. And so the bees were busy at their work, gathering all of the food that they needed to make their honey. And the bee said, 'Well, perhaps we can share some honey, but I wonder if there's something that you could share with us. What do you think you could trade us, Pooh Bear and Hattie?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'd say some nuts.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Some nuts? Oh, that sounds very nice. Now Hattie, I noticed that you're wearing a very nice hat. I've never had a hat, said the bee. Do you think you could make a very small hat for me?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, you can think about it, and I'll just give you some honey right now. How about that? And the bee said 'just a moment' and buzzed right into his hive and then there was a clink clink clonk and then the bee buzzed back out again holding two tiny little mugs. And inside of each mug was something shiny and golden and sweet. A tiny little bit of honey. And the bee said, 'Okay, here's one for you Pooh Bear and here's one for you Hattie. Would you like to taste it?'

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. Taste taste. Taste taste.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so Hattie sipped her honey. Hmm, I like it. And what did Pooh Bear do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He sipped his honey.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He sipped his honey too. And so the bee said, 'Well, I'm glad you like our honey. But what do you think about the idea of a tiny hat for a bee? Do you think I could wear a hat? What do you think, Winnie the Pooh?'

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Do you like to wear hats yourself, Winnie the Pooh?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Wait, there's something to feel under my bottom.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, I don't know, you were sitting on the wood floor. Does it hurt?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Dad, I sit on the rug.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: The rug is a little softer, yeah. And the bee said to Hattie, 'I have an idea. Maybe you could make me a tiny flower hat'. And what did Hattie say?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I make my pet bunny.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah, you've got an Agnes bunny, that's a nice bunny. And so Hattie the Mouse said, 'Hmm, let's look around. Perhaps we can make you a tiny hat out of a flower.' And so Hattie explored in the field near the beehive and she found a tiny pink flower and a tiny purple flower and a tiny blue flower. And she said, 'Hmm, perhaps one of these would be to your liking as a hat, Mr. Bee.' And the bee said, 'Perhaps I'll try one.' And which one did the bee pick?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hm, maybe I pick the yellow and brown one.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, the yellow and brown one, yes. That one suits me because I myself am a yellow creature, and the yellow hat which is also a little bit brown looks like it will go with the rest of my colors. And so the bee tried it on and said, 'Ah yes, this hat fits very nicely. Thank you very much for making this lovely flower hat for me', said the bee. 'Well, have a good day, I have much work to do making honey.' And so the bee wearing his little yellow flower hat flew back into the hive and said goodbye.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Now let's go up for potty hie!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, yes of course.

characters.name: Hattie
characters.description: A clever mouse milliner and engineer of whimsy, she is a resourceful spirit who crafts exquisite hats and soulful portraits within her hollow tree stump workshop. Though a formidable presence famously counted among the thirty legendary cats, she is currently confined to her bed by illness, her usual treks through saturated woodlands for rare tape and lollipops temporarily halted. Even in her malaise, her sharp mind lingers on a recent, stinging disagreement with Dimitar over a fish, a rare shadow cast upon a heart otherwise known for its boundless generosity and creative light.

characters.name: Mistress Mouse
characters.description: A polite three-year-old mouse and resourceful shopkeeper, this community cornerstone balances a bold artistic spirit with sharp mechanical grit. Whether crafting intricate Lego creations in her sunroom or piloting her signature pink tow truck to solve puzzles and assist neighbors, she is a master of the road and a multi-talented creator. From famously adorning cars with blue stripes to dominating the race track, her inventive soul is always on the move. Usually seen in a mushroom-adorned cardboard hat with an orange sparkle purse, this resourceful driver remains an essential, evocative fixture of every neighborhood adventure.

characters.name: Winnie the Pooh
characters.description: A hungry yet generous soul, this bear shares his honey and ladder with Hattie on their quest for the perfect golden harvest. By stilling his heart to hear the world’s quiet whispers, he has mastered the art of patient reflection, teaching his companions that even the greatest obstacles yield to the gentle power of waiting and thoughtful stillness.

characters.name: Christopher Robin
characters.description: The master of the house at the final destination is a gentle boy and companion to Tigger, recently returned from a fishing trip to serve as the catalyst for the pilgrimage to the Wizard’s castle. Navigating the halls in a small car, he wanders the gallery of existence with a quiet, unsettling grace, pausing as a helpful friend to assist in inflating balloons. A reverent guest in his own domain, he visits Piglet—the foundational architect of a looming "big bad"—before setting the gears of the quest in motion and retiring home for a nap.

characters.name: Piglet
characters.description: A diminutive and loyal companion, this small, thoughtful creature balances high-spirited play with Christopher Robin against a dedicated role in the group’s collaborative drawing sessions. Ever-attentive and earnest, he now channels his quiet focus into a heartfelt quest: discovering the perfect birthday gift for a cherished friend.

characters.name: The Bee
characters.description: A tiny, yellow insect who shares honey from his hive in exchange for a custom-made flower hat.

characters.name: Agnes
characters.description: A pet bunny mentioned as a companion to the storyteller.

places.name: Workshop
places.description: Tucked at the edge of her sprawling backyard, Hattie’s home and creative sanctuary are nestled within the weathered heart of a hollow tree stump. This cozy, sun-dappled workspace blends domestic warmth with artistic industry, creating an evocative haven where the mouse brings her visions to life amidst the scent of aged bark and wild clover.

places.name: Mudroom
places.description: The area in Hattie's workshop where she stores her colorful rain gear.

places.name: Grocery Store
places.description: A distant neighborhood shop stocked with soda, crisp celery, and jewel-toned pomegranates, forever etched in the trio’s memory for the bananas they gathered and the messy remains of squished raspberries. It remains a whimsical local sanctuary where, amidst the nostalgia, even the resident mice can be found purchasing small bowls of hearty vegetable soup.

places.name: Front Bench
places.description: A pretty little bench outside Hattie's house used for quiet listening and observation.

places.name: Wildflower Field
places.description: A vast meadow filled with purple, pink, blue, and yellow flowers where bees collect nectar.

places.name: Beehive
places.description: A ball-shaped nest nestled inside a bush of pink flowers.
